channel.title: Transcripts: Glenn Diesen
channel.description: YouTube video transcripts from Glenn Diesen

item.title: Brian Berletic: Iran War - A Gateway to War with China & Russia
item.description:
Brian Berletic is a former US Marine, author, international relations expert and host of the New Atlas. Berletic discusses how the US war against Iran is a gateway to war with China and Russia.
Follow Brian Berletic on The New Atlas: https://www.youtube.com/@TheNewAtlas
Follow Prof. Glenn Diesen: Substack: https://glenndiesen.substack.com/ X/Twitter: https://x.com/Glenn_Diesen Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/glenndiesen
Support the research by Prof. Glenn Diesen: PayPal: https://www.paypal.com/paypalme/glenndiesen Buy me a Coffee: buymeacoffee.com/gdieseng Go Fund Me: https://gofund.me/09ea012f
Books by Prof. Glenn Diesen: https://www.amazon.com/stores/author/B09FPQ4MDL
item.content:encoded:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4rHhRNaH9LI

Transcript

Welcome back. We are joined today by Brian Berettic, a former US Marine, a political analyst, author, and also host of the new Atlas. So, it's been a while. Uh, thank you very much for coming back on the program. >> Thank Thank you so much for having me back. >> So, I um we often found oursel a little bit on the different sides of the fence. I think I was a little bit more optimistic about Trump than you. that is uh I I'm I I kind of bought into some of the rhetoric because it seemed simply that this was in the interest of the United

States to adjust to the multipolar distribution of power. Again, the political realists, you know, we tend to see this is what rational states do to optimize their own security. And indeed, if the US would continue attempting to restore US hedgeimonyy in a multipolar distribution of power, one would assume that the US would exhaust itself. It would be collectively balanced by the other great powers. It would invite huge wars. In contrast, if the US would pull back and be won among many centers of power, it would have an opportunity to

recover its strength. Uh make America great again, some might say. Uh also, the large Europe Eurasian powers would at least soft balance each other to make sure that one doesn't become too powerful compared to the rest. uh and of course the US could avoid all these wars which uh also the society has become grown quite weary of. So I I was optimistic about Trump for this reason that is that he kept making the point that uh he would no longer have any wars uh he would restore fiscal responsibility and indeed when this new

national security strategy came out in December it more or less confirmed these ideas that is uh if America can't be everywhere in the multipolar world it should focus on the home base which is the western hemisphere and east Asia where its main peer competitor is but in other words they have to reduce its presence in Europe and the Middle East but a year plus we're still into this. US is still a leading actor in the war in Ukraine in terms of yeah the logistics, weapons, intelligence, targeting and of course now Trump has taken Middle East wars on steroids. This is Iran of course

is can't even be compared to countries like Iraq uh and of course setting the whole region on fire. So I is this uh I guess how are you sessing now what is going on especially with then the Middle East. Is this uh was this always destined to be or was Trump pulled into this? How do you see the American strategy? Because is this essentially now America first becoming restoring hedgeimonyy instead or what do you make of all of this? >> Because again fortunately you were right

and I was not. So, just to add this point, >> if if it makes you feel any better, I desperately wish that I was wrong about all of these things because as we can see, there is a global crisis taking shape that's going to affect every single one of us, you and I, our families, our friends, our communities, and no matter who is watching this, almost certainly they will begin feeling the effects of all of this as well. Now, unfortunately, I was right and I warned people months before the 2024 election that it didn't matter who was elected because it it is all about the structural nature of the US system. It

is controlled by large corporate financeier interests. They are predicated on the constant expansion of profit and power. That is what they do in terms of a business and that is what they do in terms of a a na nation state system that they have captured and control and have done so for decades and decades. And I constantly refer back to the policy papers which wrote this out in excruciating detail years and years ago especially this conflict taking taking place against Iran right now. I always refer to the witch which which

path to Persia Brookings Institution paper from 2009 and chapter by chapter word for word the United States from the Bush Jr. administration all the way up to this current Trump administration they have they have executed every single option that they laid out and it wasn't about any single one option being used and succeeding against Iran. They always assumed that they would have to be used in conjunction with one another, creating synergies between them and uh weakening, hollowing out and then toppling Iran in the same way that the

US has done to other countries. Syria at the end of the Biden administration, which by the way deliberately set the stage for this current war taking place under the Trump administration. I warned the moment Syria collapsed and the US and Israel were taking out Syria's integrated air defense systems that they were clearing an air corridor that was called for by that Brookings paper all the way back in 2009. They were obsessed with clearing out air corridors so that they could strike Iran directly. And as soon as they did that, you saw uh first Israel strike at the the consulate

in Damascus. That was kind of a a test run. And then there was a missile exchange there. And then in 2025, that is when this this all began. And again, the same policy paper all the way back in 2009 talked about using Israel as a means of provoking this war, getting it started in the first place, doing all of these things to prepare for it, and then using Israel as a means of triggering it to both absorb most of the retaliation and the blame for the the conflict. Again, I I I just want to impress upon

people that was all the way back in 2009. And you can see the current Trump administration uh Trump supporters, the Western media doing this exact uh technique that was laid out in this policy paper. President Trump blaming Israel for striking the the energy production sites in Iran. This was obviously a joint US-Israeli operation, but they're blaming Israel just as the policy papers laid out. So understanding the structural material reality of how the United States operates as a nation

state or as as a captured state of corporate finance or interests will give you infinite insight into what it's doing and why. And essentially it is a giant geopolitical virus expanding for profit and power which is why it could never ever accept a multipolar world because then it would mean constraining its constant need to expand power and profit which which is just fundamentally against its nature and an impossibility as long as the interests running the U. US right now continue running the US. So unfortunately this was all inevitable.

That's why uh people could just go back to my videos actually years ago warning about all of this. And that's why it it has unfolded in the way that it has. Not because I'm I'm able to predict the future, but because the corporations run US foreign and domestic policy, they come to a consensus using these policy papers. The policy papers are taken by lawyers and turned into bills, but also strategists and turned into war plans in the Pentagon and in Washington. And then the corporate media sells sells this policy to the public and tries to

convince them corporate interests are also American interests. And this is this is why it never ends. No matter who you vote for because ultimately all of these these politicians, they got into power in the first place through huge lobbying money and campaign donations mostly coming from large corporations and and entire blocks of industries. Yeah, this is um well I guess it's important to see this in the wider context because geopolitics um as you know is how geography impacts politics

uh in the in in geopolitics the main focus has always been Eurasia since at least Mackander's days and uh they always been the focus then on these three main powers on the the Russians the Chinese and the south of course the Iranians and uh it does appear we entered a a new game Because as you just said that they they always look for some synergies. Uh you know if you weaken one pull push them apart. Um and uh yeah so so you know that you can see in the war planning that there there is an efforts

to to do to see all of this um yeah combined. And in this new great game, however, we see that there's something very different than what you saw in the 19th or the 20th century, which is the desperation though, the risk takingaking, the willingness to engage even in self harm, the willingness to throw away all rules, uh essentially embrace complete anarchy as we see now with the energy markets and the preparedness to destroy the global economy. This is something new though it feels. Uh but do do you see this uh um do you see the the attempt to destroy

Iran as also then being an effort to like a stepping another stepping stone to go after China, Russia similar as as you said it was quite predictable when Iran was taken down that Iran that uh is sorry the Syria was taken down that Iran will be next. I mean you you said you don't need to you know predict the future. You can read the documents from the think tanks and all these intelligence agencies which essentially run some of them some of them some of them but but also people like General Wesley Clark who kind of outlined exactly the scenario when he outlined

the seven countries to take out with Iran being the you know the grand finale. Uh so you know this is it's hardly a secret anymore that um how how it's set up but you see do you see in sorry Iran then being a stepping stone towards the Chinese or the Russians or both? >> Both and as a matter of fact if you look all the way back to 2011 and the so-called Arab Spring which was actually engineered by the US years in advance and there is documented evidence proving all of this. The whole point of the Arab Spring was to reconfigure the Arab world

into a united front against Iran, to encircle, contain, and then collapse Iran. And then the goal always was and and if people think back to 2011, people like John McCain and uh probably Lindsey Graham and and that that circle of people, they all openly publicly and repeatedly said the Arab Spring is taking down the Arab world today. It's going after Iran tomorrow, and the final destination is Moscow and Beijing. They were very open about this and you can see from then until now the very deliberate coordinated steps taken

spanning administrations Republican or Democratic did not matter and they did it step by step by step uh tangling Russia up in Ukraine again going to the Rand Corporation paper from 2019 extending Russia that was all about weakening and tying up Russia and if you look in that paper they talk about targeting everything that we now see them targeting uh Russian energy exports, Russian energy production, tying them up in a proxy war inside of Ukraine, uh causing trouble all along their

periphery, but they also put in options in there like sending more weapons to Syria because Russia was desperately trying to stabilize Syria. And and then we can see that eventually all of these put together on on Russia weakened and distracted uh Russia. They were forced to make a decision. They decided Ukraine was the priority. Syria collapsed. But as you can see, Syria was in itself a stepping stone towards encircling, containing and and launching war against Iran. And of course, all of this is aimed at China. The United

States knows that China is on the rise that it probably has a five five to 10 year 10 years at absolute most likely somewhere around 5 years. China will irreversibly surpass the United States in ways that will make it impossible for the United States to ever try to slow down or stop its rise. in particular in terms of energy independence, China. If people look at what China has been doing over the last several years, in addition to the Belt and Road uh initiative to create land routes to circumvent potential maritime oil blockades, which

China has always been aware of and preparing for, they themselves domestically have been ramping up uh coal to to liquid uh industrial processes to take coal and turn it into an alternative for the fuel they're still importing from the Middle East. They have been building these pipelines with Russia, importing huge amounts of energy into China from Russia. And they plan on bringing in even more. There's been a rush for nuclear power and of course solar and other renewable power. This is by far the largest adopter of

all of these uh sources of energy than anyone else on earth. Nuclear, renewables, doesn't matter. China is in the lead by far. And that is because they are trying to achieve energy independence. And it was probably around 2030 they would have achieved that. And so the United States had a very tiny closing window of opportunity to try to disrupt China before they made that final transition. This is why they've struck at Iran. And you can see what has happened. Not only are they not only did they attack Iran, but before that they invaded, kidnapped the president of Venezuela and is now holding hostage the

remaining Venezuelan government. They have stopped energy from flowing from Venezuela to China. They are, as the New York Times has reported, and as was always obvious all along, the CIA is behind these long range drone strikes deep into Russian territory, striking Russian energy production. And the CIA is also using these maritime drones to strike at tankers carrying Russian energy. In addition to that, and for years, the US has been sabotaging Belt and Road Initiative uh infrastructure in Myammar, in Pakistan. in Myammar. It is a pipeline as moving hydrocarbons across

Myamar to China has been attacked many times by US backed militants. Uh so as you can see the US has already been setting up what is essentially a global maritime oil blockade on China. And if you look at their plans spanning many years, they always assumed that they would want to do it in the Mala Strait and in the Asia-Pacific region because if they did that, they would still be able to export energy from the Middle East to all of their proxies and keep their global network of proxies viable while only strangling, selectively

strangling China. But that is not possible anymore because of China's military rise. Uh people say, "Where is China's military? Why aren't they helping Iran? It's in the Asia-Pacific region. This is why the US cannot strangle China in the Asia-Pacific region. And so they've decided instead of doing that and because of things that myself, you and many others have been talking about in terms of the US military-industrial base, its inability to produce the amount of munitions it requires for its global war against I would say multipolarism and ultimately China. They don't have the munitions to take out Iran, continue pressuring

Russia, and also fight a large-scale war against China anywhere in the Asia-Pacific proxy or directly. And so I honestly believe that they have now done is attempt to solve two problems with one war, try to destabilize, topple Iran, and also create the conditions in the region that ultimately start choking exports from the Middle East and then close them off alto together. people and this this is the last thing I will say about this. People are saying that tankers are getting through Iran is letting tankers go through to China. That may be true. But as we see the US

and Israel have struck energy production in Iran because that is going to impact the energy Iran is able to export to China. There have been attacks on energy production in Qatar, in Kuwait, in Saudi Arabia. All of these places are shutting down production. cut or shut down all of its LNG production that that that contributed to the exports going to China. So even if the ships can get through because Iran is letting them what are they carrying the production is down to zero or reduced to 50%. So this is already taking shape into a global uh

oil blockade on China and this is the one last area Russia uh China was receiving these exports from and the US is incrementally closing it down and the way they're doing it is acting as if we had no plan and this is spiraling out of control and it just so happens that it's it's cutting China off slowly but surely and we're bringing more assets into the region to keep the war going and and make it worse and worse Because if they openly said, "We're going to impose a a global maritime blockade on China under international law is an act of war." And

they're trying to avoid that. >> Yeah. I often make the point that the the the geoeconomics, if you will, of China, that it has a lot in common with what was the American system in the in the beginning of the 19th century. That is when the Americans wanted the economic independence from the British, they they built the American system on these three pillars. They wanted manufacturing that is the technology and industry. They wanted the transportation uh under control which was you know roads and ports and later on became railroad and of and the third was the financial power the a national bank and

this kind of laid the economics for sustaining American independence after yeah after freeing themselves from the from the British. But the the the Chinese are more or less following a similar path though they have the same three pillars. They they have the the the techn the technological leadership and industrial base. They have this transport corridor essentially a belt and road initiative connecting the world. And they have also this um financial instruments of power. There's uh internationalizing their currency,

new payment system, development banks, all of this. And it looks as if this is where the US is what the US has been trying to go after though. That is to roll back its again the US very open about this. So again as he says part of the conspiracy theory you know we rolled back its technological development disrupted supply chains to undermine his uh industrial power uh disconnected from energy sources like oil as we see now Venezuela um and uh yeah shut off this transport transportation corridors which is also important uh Trump is very open about the preparedness of uh to punish any

country in bricks who try to ddollarize again undermining this alternative ative development banks. So across the board, this is what's been done. But the main reason it doesn't work is because none of these countries stand alone anymore. You have this Eurasian powers cooperating. And the the I guess the the concern is if the US does not get its um get to achieve its objective through economic warfare then it goes it will um well transition into uh a military approach instead where allout war is uh the the only alternative to sustain

dominance. uh it uh yeah this is it's hard to you know not consider that possibility now with this massive war uh spreading very very fast. Um but I did want to ask you though about how you see the possibility now for diplomacy because uh you know it's often pointed out that diplomacy and even when the US speaks about peace it's often deceptive and uh as you saw of course there's been two surprise attacks on Iran during these negotiations but it's also about escalation control because you mentioned

the south par the largest gas field in the world which the US attacked I see only a few hours ago Trump came out on Twitter or proof social and he posted that, you know, oh, we we didn't want this. We don't want an energy war. It was Israel. Uh, let's not, you know, do this to the poor consumer. However, if Iran retaliates, then we'll burn everything down. I mean, this is what you do for escalation control. You escalate a bit and then you convince the other side not to do the same and then you can go up and down and punish without them retaliating. It just seems this is something that Iran, if it

accepts, is finished. But but do you see any links then to Russia? Because this is also what the US is doing there. As you say, they keep talking about peace, but they're also behind the attacks on Russian ships. They're they're still doing the targeting within Russia. They're still supplying the weapons. I mean, this is uh is there any point anymore to this diplomacy or is it pure now deception for the purpose of escalation dominance? >> Very long question. I >> No, it's a good question. It's an important question and the answer is the

US has no interest in diplomacy. It wants primacy over the planet. Nobody. It will nobody's going to accept that. Everyone is going to attempt in their own way to resist against it. And what the United States uses diplomacy to do and what it has used diplomacy for specifically against Iran is to advance continuity of agenda in a way that is not openly obvious. They want it to look like they're trying to be reasonable and these are just unfortunate developments or unfortunate breakdowns in diplomacy that forces them to to uh resort to

military force when in reality they're not using diplomacy in any shape, form or way to try to avoid war. They are deliberately using it as a pretext for war. And again, not not to uh uh beat this horse to death, but the the Wish Path to Persia paper back in 2009 had a whole chapter about diplomacy. And if you read through the chapter, they basically say this this option is the best option for launching a a regime change war against Iran because it'll convince the world that we wanted

diplomacy and it was Iran that brought this all upon themselves. And so they that is that is the game that they're playing. They're also if anyone in any of these capitals actually buys into what the US is saying in terms of diplomacy, they're they're also deceiving their opponents in in this game. But ultimately it is to create a pretext uh not prevent war. So this is why I think Russia's playing its own game. China also playing its own game. I don't know about Iran, but but I would say probably almost certainly they were just trying to buy time playing their

own game. I think they're all well aware of this. This is why Russia has never made any sort of concession at all. The diplom diplomacy that they've engaged in with the United States, they've never made any concessions at all because they know it's just going to be Minsk 3.0 even if everyone in the US government swears and promises that it won't be. >> Yeah. Well, I actually saw well right before the US invaded there was reports in Politico where um well, it said that uh people in the White House or Pentagon so someone they interviewed at least

made the made a point that it would be better if an attack on Iran if Israel would attack first because then when Iran retaliates then the US can help come to Israel's assistance or you know protect its you know its right to exist or you know this in in in the short term just then it could be sold as a defensive war essentially. So this was seen as ideal because one has to manage the narrative as well. Uh eventually didn't go with this option. It it could probably have been better. Um but uh

yeah I wanted to yeah shift a bit to the more specifics what's happening in Iran now because this seems like a war intended to exhaust each other. that is both sides want to bring pain to the other and they also have to be able to absorb the pain. Uh what do we know though so far about the uh the capabilities of each side? The extent to which it has been degraded over the past uh oh yeah we're in the third week now I think. >> Yes almost three weeks almost three full weeks and uh so so this is what the the

US is doing. They tried a a very quick uh overwhelming attack hoping that maybe maybe it would cause a a chain reaction and everything would unravel. But I don't think by by any stretch of the imagination they did not have a plan B. They they had to have known. Uh mosaic defense is something that is mentioned throughout policy papers spanning decades in in in the collective west and in the US specifically. So they had to have known how resilient uh Iranian command and control was. how decentralized it was. They already waged

this at least the uh an early phase of this war. I would connect the the violence last year against Iran to the conflict taking place right now as one single war of aggression just being launched in phases with brief pauses in between to to re reorganize, regroup, rearm. So they had to have known that. And so what they're trying to do now is wear Iran down economically. uh they're they're starting to hit infrastructure that is essential for the civilian population of Iran, 93 million people.

They depend on on exporting hydrocarbons. Half of the half of government revenue comes from those exports. And so the US attacking that and then of course blaming Israel for it, that is threatening the the livelihoods of 93 million people in Iran. It is not about just targeting the government or the military. Uh but again I I honestly believe this is also about just closing the straight of Hormuz. I believe uh one of the possibilities of this marine expeditionary unit making its way to the Middle East right now from the

Asia-Pacific region is to engage in interdiction of ships that are allowed to pass through the straight of Hormuz. I believe the United States at least wants to have the capability of doing that in the area. And that is exactly one of the capabilities the the Marine Expeditionary Unit gives the US in in that region. And so I I believe they want to continue the war, but I want I I believe they want it to continue to spiral out of control and very gradually and in an apparently natural way just close off all exports one way or the other. mutually assured uh destruction

of each other's energy production is already happening. It's already reducing the amount of exports going going to China. Uh they want to topple the Iranian government. That is a prerequisite for isolating Russia and China and and then eventually working on Russia and China uh on their own without their allies and partners. But I think it's also about cutting China off. So we have to just keep an eye on that. The problem the US has is they have a very limited amount of anti-missile munitions. We've been

talking about this for years in regards to the the proxy war against Russia and Ukraine. Uh even before the the conflict in Ukraine began in 2022 or escalated in 2022, there was already a shortage of Patriot missiles because of the US proxy war against Yemen being fought through Saudi Arabia. Saudi Arabia was running out of out of Patriot missiles and the US wasn't able to supply them anymore at that time. They had to borrow from their neighbors. And so that was the situation all the way back then. Imagine how bad it is now with the the conflict in Yemen continuing, the conflict last year, the

the war of aggression launched against Iran last year and now this year. They're running very low on these interceptors. And you can see that Iran has begun pacing its missile launches every day about 20 30 launches a day. They're not out of missiles. Uh they're still launching them in a at a steady rate and more of more of them are getting through. So what does that mean? It means that the air defense munitions are are running out and there's gaps in the air defenses. We saw Iran taking out extremely valuable, I would say almost

practically irreplaceable radars for the THAAD system. These are I think there's like 13 in all of existence and three to five of them are already destroyed in in just this fighting alone. So that is significant. So the US is working against all of that. It's it's got all of these warplanes it's bringing in. has naval vessels that are all either already hitting maintenance walls or going to hit maintenance walls. We see the USS Ford essentially retreating to create to get repaired. And I highly doubt the damage that is being described on the USS Ford can be repaired at that

facility. They're almost certainly going to have to go back to the United States and get repaired. So that's what they're run. They're they're overstretched. They want to wage this global war against multipolarism, but they don't have the infinite resources to do it. Multipolarism in and of itself continues to grow and expand and get stronger. And there's just this inflection point where the US is going to end up exhausted, overextended like all empires before it. And we'll have to wait and see what what

they then do. What will they do? they have nuclear weapons, which is something the British Empire didn't have when it when it reached its limits. What is the United States going to try to do at that inflection point? Uh what will happen if Iran does begin to to atrophy and topple because its own military capabilities are exhausted? Uh I don't know. It's it's it is a very dangerous situation because ultimately it's all aimed at both Russia and China which are both two nuclear armed nation states. Uh so there's just no good way for this

conflict to to to go actually at this point. >> In terms of the the the capabilities of the United States, um you mentioned all these radars which are being destroyed. You know several of them have cost hundreds of millions and more importantly the the the resources required to build them. You need for example gallium to build this and uh 98 or 99% of the gallium production happens to occur in China which means if

the US wants to continue this war heading towards China it needs the help of China and uh well one sees the problems already but of course it's much wider if uh you know this seems like a lot of people in the United States are waking up to the the the challenges of not having any of these rare earth minerals and uh you know if you want the artillery the missiles you also need the antimony again all with China as well and the the Chinese appear to be have woken up to this and um well not the demilitarized the US but they would like

to to well they put caps and the limits and on on these um minerals which can be used for war given that the US uh well it war machine is obviously targeting I think China as well. So what does all this tell you though and the intentions of the United States what it wants to do? I think that's kind of clear for you know anyone who reads all these policy papers especially out of these uh think tanks like Rand but but to what extent you think these intentions can be met

with the actual capabilities because I don't think the the US probably planned the all the difficulties is facing now. I do believe that yeah from Biden to Trump when they boast about you know how with the greatest country most powerful military in the world we can take on everyone at the same time almost a direct quote from Biden by the way uh then uh then um you know to what extent do you think they overestimated their capabilities here and what happens when they run into a wall >> I I don't think that they actually over

overestimated their own abilities I think they understand where where the deficiencies lie. And I think that's why we're seeing what is obviously a very rushed process. They're not even they're not even trying to dress any of this up anymore. You have people like President Trump saying, "We're going to take Cuba." Who who talks like that? That's like a a movie villain talks like that. You don't see real leaders in the real world talking like that because it's so blatant, so inappropriate, so obviously illegal and wrong on all levels. But

they're not even trying to conceal what it is that they're doing because that is what they're doing. They're just seizing nations. They are at war with multipolarism. The message to all other nations in the world investing in multiparism right now, which is a growing list of countries. They're saying, "If you continue to do this, we will do this to you." What we've just done to Venezuela. We will kidnap your president. We'll kill hundreds of people in your capital city, and we'll hold your government hostage. and we will strip mine the resources out from under your feet and there's nothing you can do about it. And that is again something

else President Trump, people in his administration, people in the in the Western media have said there's nothing you can do to stop it. It's it's this open global thugy. It's it's mafioso almost. And uh that's this is the whole message they're trying to send to the world by knocking over Cuba. They've got these lowhanging fruit they think they can make examples out of to try to scare everyone else back into line. They want to try to strangle the Chinese economy, undermine Chinese economic growth to try to scare them maybe back into line or at least give them a little bit more time

on the clock because ultimately they're out of time. Uh again, I said China is about five years from irreversibly surpassing the US in in all metrics and in ways the US can never can never ever stop or reverse. And this is this is what they're racing against. And from their point of view, I mean, honestly, look at it. Look at the the US uh the corporate financier elites even the the political class. Have any of them been held accountable for anything ever since Bush just let's talk about the 21st century from Bush Junior all the way up

until now. No. Who's paying the price for all of this? People in the Middle East who are being killed uh including American soldiers by the way. They're they're also being killed in all of this. uh consumers all around the globe who are watching fuel prices rise and then the prices of everything else dependent on fuel rise. They're the ones all paying the price for this. Not the United States, not not Washington, not Wall Street. They're not paying the price. They see no point on not trying this last last ditch gamble to try to reassert primacy over the planet. And

they may even go as far as believing just like during World War II, we can flatten Eurasia in in a gigantic continentalwide conflict or even beyond continentalwide conflict. We can flatten it and we will emerge virtually untouched because we have two oceans between us. Just like World War II, we will come out the other end after everyone is exhausted by our wars and our proxy wars. and that this is what they think then maybe we have a chance to reassert ourselves. Maybe we will come out on the

other side of this stronger relative to say China. It's their only hope because as things are right now, China is inevitably going to surpass the US and the entire collective west and there and unless they do something drastic, there is nothing else they can do to stop it. >> So how long do you think then this war could last? because uh you know Trump made the point that they will start to hit harder but at the same time he also recognizes that there's not that many other any many many more military targets to hit. So if one wants to

escalate one can go in other ways. One can of course go after the civilian population. So the hospitals uh critical infrastructure to put pressure on the people. Uh one can go after as we saw now the gas fields, the oil uh to cause an energy crisis. Uh economic problems within uh one can also attack nuclear power plants. Uh we saw that now with the attack on the Busher nuclear power plant and uh you know this like a little nuclear attack. Of course it could also go into an actual nuclear attack. So what do you see what does going up the

escalation ladder mean now for you think for the United States and how long can this last? >> Yeah. So in the very beginning the resources that they started this war of aggression against Iran with that that was probably like two or three weeks they could have gone real intense like that. But you could see even maybe a few days or maybe a week before they reached that limit they were already bringing in additional resources. they were already bringing in another carrier. They were flying war planes from the US to Europe and then bringing them into the region. And so now they probably had resources

to have this drag on another one or two months, maybe even longer uh at at at a very intense tempo. And of course, if they feel they've degra degraded Iranian capabilities, but they just can't destabilize and topple the government, they can have a very reduced tempo and they can maintain that almost indefinitely, especially if they're able to fly even over just sections of Iran without really worrying that much about Iranian air defenses. They they could keep this going for a very very long time. Uh again, that their limits are

the interceptors and Iran's ability to constantly fire ballistic missiles every single day. Uh if if they significantly run low and there's huge gaps in in interceptors and Iran really begins dismantling the US military infrastructure across the region, which includes Israel's essentially a a unsinkable US aircraft carrier just pushed up right against the region. uh it could they could start dismantling that and causing real real trouble. Of course, the US can move its resources, its its assets back and and launch

attacks from a distance, but every time you you move further back, you're putting greater maintenance strain on your aircraft, your refueling capabilities and everything like that. So, there's a lot of dilemmas. I think you hit on a a really good point. The US is already striking at civilian infrastructure, economic essential economic infrastructure. They will continue to do that and I'm I'm almost certain that they're going to start stopping tankers that Iran is allowing to go through the the straight of Hormus. They will almost certainly start seizing that and it c it can escalate out of control. The thing that I worry

about the most is the way you the the very blatant way the US uses Israel as the just the al it's just the ultimate scapegoat. they can do anything through Israel and then convince large sections of the public that it was Israel and there was nothing the US could do to stop them and that it goes up to and including nuclear weapons. They have nuclear weapons. If the US wanted to use nuclear weapons on Iran for some reason, they would just have Israel do it. And then President Trump would just sit there on Truth Social and say, "I told them not to do it. I didn't know they

were going to do it and they just did it." Again, there's a whole chapter in the witch path to Persia paper from 2009 titled Leave it to BB, and it's all about using Israel as a disposable proxy to advance US interests in a in a regime change operation against Iran. And so, I I do believe those are all possibilities, which is why this conflict is so dangerous. But again, the the US is in this rush. They are desperate. A desperate wounded empire is the the most dangerous empire imaginable. >> No, I agree. I think uh yeah they're not

going to go go down without swinging but um u but in terms of how long this can go on I'm thinking or where it might break on either side because Iran could break the US could break as well but um but we we don't only measure this in military capabilities. So it's also about um the ability to weaken the economics of the other side. Uh the political cohesion uh the societal support um I mean for example the resignation by Joe Kent I thought this

was quite significant in terms of uh yeah undermining public support for the war. But how do you see it happening on both sides? How how capable are they ofstanding it? If we look besides the actual military capabilities and uh you know again the economics and political and well social political issues >> well on Iran's side they they have been very resilient for decades and decades. The eight-year war was a tremendous tragedy for the Iranian nation imposed

on it again by the US through Iraq and they were able to survive through that and then move past that and and that was again that was eight years I just hundreds of thousands of people dying on on both sides for eight years straight. Tremendous damage dealt to Iran to its economy to its energy production infrastructure. they were losing ships. Uh and so they were a able to weather that then they've weathered tremendous pressure by the US ever since then because this has been a constant process

of trying to wear down and topple Iran since the the late 1970s since the Iranians rightfully ousted the the US client regime that was installed on top of them. Uh and so they're very resilient but there there's always limits. We don't know where those limits are. They they have already demonstrated tremendous resilience throughout this this current phase of this war. How much longer can they go? Especially if the US begins dismantling the the economic uh they they already

hit water supplies. They could start hitting energy production. Widescale blackouts uh energy production also energy production. all of these things, uh, all kinds of infrastructure, hospitals, they already blew up a girl's school and then and then lied about it and and then blamed it on Iran. Uh, whether it was deliberate or not, this is what happens when you wage a war of aggression. Things like that happen. So whether you deliberately meant to do that or not, it was things like that are going to happen, which is why you don't launch a war of aggression in the first place. So

they're they're responsible for all of that and that is what they're doing to Iran. When does that actually break Iran? I don't know. in terms of US political cohesion. I mean, again, this is what I was war warning about before the 2024 elections. There is no opposition. The the Democratic party, the Republican party, everybody in between in Washington is is working for Wall Street. And no matter what theater they perform in front of the American public, they're always going to work for Wall Street. This is what they've always

been doing. This is why from George Bush Jr., You watch them go step by step by step up towards this war and one administration setting the stage perfectly for the next administration to to advance towards this war of aggression against Iran that's now taking place. Uh the Joe Kent resigning I would be very careful about this because the whole purpose of him resigning was not to criticize the Trump administration. It was to blame Israel. So again go back to that policy paper from 2009. Leave it to BB. use Israel as a disposable proxy to execute your war of aggression against Iran. This is a

war that may go badly for the United States. We don't know. We don't know at this point. I think it's still too early to tell. It's definitely, no matter which way it goes, destroying the global economy. And so if you could, wouldn't you want to blame that on somebody else and and flush all of that down with somebody else and then emerge on the other side of this with your hands relatively clean or at least some people believing you you're relatively clean from all of this? So that's that's what he is doing. He he has even even when he was condemning this and and blaming Israel, he was still acting as if Iran

was some sort of threat or they were they were killing Americans uh for years and years, but he doesn't mention that these are Americans that were illegally invading the region specifically to encircle and contain and threaten Iran's own existence. If it was the other way around, Americans would would have done the exact same thing. He doesn't talk about all of that. So he is an establishment figure doing damage control and scapegoating. So I'd be very careful about this and and and the American political system is designed specifically to compartmentalize uh damage control, scapegoat, divide,

distract and continue of agenda. That is what it is designed to do. It is very good at doing that. >> In terms of uh lacking proper position, I think uh yeah that's one of the lessons of this war as well. Trump, I mean, he seemed he was the ultimate populist, the the one challenged the establishment, the political establishment, even within his own party, the the media, uh, you know, going against this forever wars, all this u, you know, all the cows you weren't allowed to touch. And u but now, of course, yeah, one gets the impression

it doesn't really matter who you vote for, it would have been the exact same policies of yeah, the the other side. Uh but how how do you see Iran playing its cards now though? Because uh again yesterday they struck uh that is the US or Israel or US Israel struck um the the south parts gas field in Iran and u throughout the night. Now as we slept we saw that uh uh Iran hit Qatar some of its gas fields. It hit uh Saudi Arabia. I thought it was interesting that it hit

the refinery in ports on Saudi Arabia's uh western coast that is on the Red Sea which then you know blocks off this side as well. So it appears to be recognizing what kind of fight it's in and sees the energy as then being the main area to reciprocate. And you can also I guess it's not unthinkable that uh Yemen would then join in and try to close off the straight to block off the Red Sea, then that would be that could bring down the

the Gulf States alto together. Do do you or given that the Iranians see the same as everyone else, how how do you think they're going to play this? Is this uh what they're going to do more or less uh just they give this ultimatum to the Gulf States? Either we, you know, shut you down completely or you decouple from America or I guess what what what what's the game the Iranians are playing here? >> Well, I mean I I can't really be sure the the problem with this if that was if that's their strategy. The problem with

that is these these Arab states are essentially politically captured. They're they're essentially in the same situation Ukraine is in. Ukraine couldn't do anything if they wanted to. They have been put in this impossible position that serves US interests completely at its own expense. And and that is what's happening to these Arab states right now. Uh all of them. Um they I don't I don't know how they could ever have possibly honestly believed that they were getting American protection and the US was a re reliable partner. Although I don't know. I mean

there could be people out there that gullible. I don't know. But they they had to have known that they were proxies and that they were always essentially riding the tiger and there was no graceful way to get off of it. And I think that's where they are right now. They they're maybe they're tempted to get off, but how do you do that? There really is no way to do that. And so Iran is trying to impose the the maximum cost on the US and its proxies in the region as much as possible. I don't think they they imagine these Arab states are going to how are they going to force the US

out. You know, think about that. People should think about how how would how exactly would they go about doing that? I mean, I'd be very surprised if it happened. If it did happen, that would be that would be great news. It would be a a revolution in in geopolitical developments, but I just don't see it happening any more than I see Ukrainians rising up and saying, "No, we don't want to be part of this proxy war anymore." think because they're politically captured from the top down that it's they're just going to continue the US is going to use them down to the last piece of infrastructure, the last the

last Arab fighting their war against Iran. And they're actually doing the same thing with Israel, too. A lot of people think this is a a reverse situation, but right now Israel is also getting hit by ballistic missiles every single day and now rockets from Hezbollah and they're they're sending their troops into Lebanon and they're unable to make progress against uh Hezbollah on the ground in southern Lebanon. And so everybody in the region because of this relationship and this servitude for the United States, they're now trapped in this war. and the and their only hope is the US being able to

finally degrade Iranian military capabilities and topple the government. That's that's really their only hope. I don't see any of them ever telling the US, "No, we don't want to do this anymore and please get out of our country." I just don't see them being able to do that. A lot a lot of the Arab states, their internal security was built up and is partially run by the United States and its and its other partners, its Western partners. I don't know how they would even begin to think about getting rid of the United States. >> Yeah. On on the Ukrainian issue, it always surprised me that all the

information, you know, is more or less accessible even in Western medias, which is from, you know, New York Times, the Washington Post, they confirmed a lot of things over the past years. Now, they confirmed many things. One was that the US captured the intelligence services from the first day after the coup. This has been confirmed. We learned from the general prosecutor in Ukraine that they are the one who picked the had to pick and get to choose everyone who was going to be in government. Uh so they captured a political system. We know that they built their own military force that is the the far-right groups were trained

armed uh by the American intelligence services and um not just to fight dissident like in Donbas but also to put a veto power on ke if they would ever think about making peace. We we know from the temporary hiccup with the defunding of the NOS's that the NGOs's financed or more or less represent now American NOS's working with the intelligence services represent the civil society in Ukraine. We know that they finance 85 to 90% of the Ukrainian media and uh so yeah it's a good point

the societies are are captured and again as we saw in 2019 when the Ukrainians could actually vote and 73% voted for peace platform how quickly that was shut down the the same NOS's uh backed up by the American trained uh right-wing groups who put down these red lines essentially all the things you run on the entire platform everything is a red you don't cross any of this and uh he reversed himself. I mean this is a captured state. So it's a it's a good point but it is it's the same rhetoric I feel that is we we still talk about you

know now we have to help uh you know Ukraine gets to decide we don't do anything we don't agree to anything unless Ukraine decides but Ukraine doesn't decide anything this is the decisions are made in uh in Washington and other places uh but it but but I guess this is my point if the Arab states do not let uh well do not decouple from the US it looks like the the Iranians could destroy them that is uh not the civilian population or the civilian population centers but if they

cut off the dissalination plants for example there's no more water they you know most of them are living in what is essentially desert so is this is this the end of the Gulf states or is that um I guess overly ambitious uh for the Iranians >> the the Iranians so far they've and they've been reciprocating. So they're not they're not doing this themselves initially. They are responding to what is being done to them. But almost certainly they have to know that what is being done to them is done knowing full well that they will reciprocate. And of

course the United States doesn't care about anyone in the Arab world or even in Israel. They don't care about that entire region. They are all proxies. They are all expendable just like Ukraine. Do does anyone honestly believe the US cares about anyone in Ukraine? anything about Ukraine except for its utility and advancing US geopolitical objectives visav Russia and in this case visa v Iran. Uh so that Iran, Russia, China, they're all in a terrible position because they are surrounded by proxies, the US ultimately sees as expendable and no matter how much damage

they do to these proxies, the source of the problem is still the United States on the other side of the planet. This is this is a huge problem. But if you could eliminate these proxies or seriously degrade these proxies, you would in a way be you'd be degrading America's ability to to menace you with these proxies. The the weaker and more destroyed they are, the the harder it is for the US to use them against the targeted nation. Uh but also just the nature of this conflict alone, the amount of munitions the US is

burning through, not just anti-missile munitions, but long range precisiong guided munitions. I I I think you remember me talking about strategic sequencing and how the US was sending weapons to Ukraine that it actually was never ever going to use in a potential war they they were going to provoke with China and the Asia-Pacific. But the weapons they're using on Iran right now are absolutely the the type of weapons they were saving for a war potential war they wanted to provoke with China in the Asia Pacific. So they're depleting their their inventory. They have no way of uh refilling these depleted stocks. It will

take them years and years. They've gone through years of Tunnelhawk cruise missiles and and Jassums. They've just gone through years of them. Years of production in just a couple of days, the opening phase of this operation. So, in a way, Iran just surviving and absorbing all of this and wearing down the the inventories of the United States seriously inhibit there. Right now, the US is on this global uh war of aggression, murder, kidnapping spree. It's just on a on a global criminal rampage. But they cannot do that

forever. And the longer Iran holds out, the more depleted the United States will be and the the less capable the US will be a be able to just go to the next targeted nation and continue the momentum of this spree that it's on right now. since uh I would say I was I would say this started at the end of the Biden administration and then it was supercharged during the the Trump administration and again it always always by plan. uh the the attack on the Iranian consulate. I mean that was the beginning of this whole process. And if you understand that this whole process

is aimed at both Russia and China at the same time as Iran, then you you can understand how serious this we can see how serious the conflict is now that this has all led to. And they knew it was they knew it was going to lead to this and they did it anyway. Uh, so at the end of the day, they're depleting themselves and they're limiting their ability to pivot from from here, no matter what the outcome is with Iran, to menace anyone else anywhere else. If there's a silver lining, >> yes. Yeah, that is a silver lining. But yeah,

not only are they depleting the resources, so the the military resources, but uh it's also creating some some shocks to countries, I guess, like South Korea seeing now all their air defenses being pulled out to be sent to Iran. I mean, at some point, you would think that the proxies or the frontline states in East Asia and Europe would look towards the Gulf States and think, oh, you know, we we could be next. But, you know, again, this might be overly optimistic. I see here in Scandinavia, you know, they are now after being, you know, neutral or semi-neutral or, you

know, at least managing the security competition cautiously for all these decades, they're now opening US military bases and very enthusiastically embracing the role as frontline states even as they are scared to death of Trump's uh, you know, erratic behavior. Uh so you know they should be looking at uh Ukraine or the Gulf states and ask you know are we going to be the next frontline states to you know fight for the empire but uh that doesn't appear to be happening not yet at least

but uh yeah as you said if this continues over a longer period of time maybe someone will begin to question some of the assumption that uh you know haven't been allowed to be questioned so far. Um anyway, any final thoughts before we wrap up? >> Yeah. Yeah. Actually, regarding that, regarding South Korea, you know, South Korea really doesn't need those interceptors. Who who are they really worried about attacking them out of the blue? Nobody. Nobody is ever going to attack South Korea. Uh North Korea and South Korea at times relations were were warming up and it took the United States

to interject into the process to to freeze it again. Uh South Korea and China do tremendous amounts of business and that's all China wants to do with South Korea is to do business with South Korea. They don't they don't want to invade. They don't want to shoot missiles at South Korea. The only reason they would need those missiles is that the US provoked the war and then South Korea as as a result of that war being provoked ended up having missiles fired at it because it's hosting US military bases exactly like the Arab states. And unfortunately, whether it's uh the Arab states, Ukraine, South Korea, Japan, the

Philippines, recently you're talking about uh Northern Europe. We're these are all politically captured that the top levels of the government have been captured. They work for Washington gladly. They know they're insulated from the consequences of all of this. They will perform theater to make it seem as if they're trying to look out for your best interests. If people start to see through this and ultimately I think that's what it's going to take. It's going to take a combination of people inside these these countries waking up to this realizing that their political system is it's it's not even broken. It's a it's a system of control, not

self-determination. And then they need to decide how do we change that? But also multipolarism continuing to build up itself faster than the US is able to destroy it. And as we're watching, the US is severely depleting itself in these wars of aggression, one right after the other after the other, multiple wars at one time. At some point, there will be an inflection point where yes, the multipolar world can build itself up faster than US uh can destroy it. But we all have to do our best to to wake people up to that. That's that's what's happening. Let's not myopically focus on

just Iran or just Ukraine or just Venezuela and Cuba. US is at war globally against multipolarism. uh to maintain its unipolarism. So we have to realize that, expose it, wake other people up to it and start thinking about what we can do to ensure that multipolarism a bal a fair balance of power around the world prevails over this abusive obviously out of control unipolar world order >> and well you would think South Korea would be aware of this because you know if you go back to after World War II in 1956 that is when the Soviet Union and

Japan were close to a peace or they signed this joint declaration they might solve the territorial issue and uh you know it's interesting uh you know in regards to what you said what happens if relations warm up and the US actually went into a panic that peace might break out because if peace breaks out that is the Soviets and Japanese make peace that is in 1956 then that undermines the foundation for the hedgemony because if you're a hedgeimmon you need the alliance systems if there's no tensions then uh the adversary can't be contained and your proxy won't remain loyal so You

need to have you need to have the simmering tension otherwise under control of course otherwise Japan would start to you know uh exercise some autonomy its own thinking and the Soviet Union wouldn't have the same containment uh on the Pacific. So it's uh you know we we've seen this movie many times before and yet we we are surprised when we see it again. So well thank you very much for taking the time though. >> Thank you so much for having me on.
